title: cracca ciceroidea
intro: Cracca ciceroidea, also known as American licorice or wild licorice, is a perennial plant native to North America with a long history of use in traditional medicine. The plant's roots contain glycosides, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cracca ciceroidea include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as reduction of pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps and other conditions. Traditionally, the plant's roots have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
